question.text: Which institution was the first financial institution (not a bank) to set up a merchant banking division in India?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** ICICI was the first financial institution to set up a merchant banking division in India in 1973. Note that SBI (a bank) had set up its division in 1972, but ICICI was the first among financial institutions. **The Trap:** IDBI is tempting because it is another major development financial institution, but it entered merchant banking much later in 1992. IFCI also entered but was not the first. **Cross-Exam Context:** The evolution of Indian financial institutions is relevant to NISM Series V-B and Series IX certifications. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember the distinction: SBI was the first public sector bank (1972), while ICICI was the first financial institution (1973). IDBI came much later (1992), post-liberalization.
